{| class="wikitable" |- |{{ill|Kurikara Fudō|en}}[[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]][[ja:倶利伽羅不動]] |}[[File:Mount Hiei from Kyoto - IMG 5701.JPG|thumb|right|300px|Mount Hiei]] '''Sannō Gongen''' (山王権現) is a {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|en}}[[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]][[ja:神仏習合]] deity that emerged from the fusion of {{ill|mountain worship|en}}[[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]][[ja:山岳信仰]] on Mount Hiei ({{ill|Mount Hiei|en}}[[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]][[ja:比叡山]]), {{ill|Shinto|en}}[[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]][[ja:神道]], and {{ill|Tendai|en}}[[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]][[ja:天台宗]] Buddhism. It serves as the {{ill|chinjugami|en}}[[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]][[ja:鎮守神|lt=guardian deity]] of the Tendai school. It was also referred to as '''Hiyoshi Gongen''' or '''Hiyoshi Sannō Gongen'''. == History == Sannō Gongen is the {{ill|chinjugami|en}}[[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]][[ja:鎮守神|lt=guardian deity]] of {{ill|Enryaku-ji|en}}[[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]][[ja:延暦寺]], formed through the fusion of {{ill|mountain worship|en}}[[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]][[ja:山岳信仰]], {{ill|Shinto|en}}[[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]][[ja:神道]], and {{ill|Tendai|en}}[[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]][[ja:天台宗]] on Mount Hiei ({{ill|Mount Hiei|en}}[[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]][[ja:比叡山]]). It is also sometimes used to refer to the {{ill|saijin|en}}[[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]][[ja:祭神|lt=deities enshrined]] at {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|en}}[[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]][[ja:日吉大社]]. Sannō Gongen, as the deity of Mount Hiei, is also affectionately called "Hiyossan" (Hiyoshi-san) and is enshrined in all [[:Category:Hiyoshi Shrines|Hiyoshi Shrines]] nationwide, with {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|en}}[[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]][[ja:日吉大社]] serving as the head shrine[http://www.rekihaku.otsu.shiga.jp/news/1104.html "Special Exhibition on Kami and Buddhas in Ōmi" (Ōtsu City Museum of History, September 3, 2011)]. The term "Hiyoshi Sannō" refers to the blended faith that regarded Mount Hiei as a "sacred mountain," integrating the belief systems of both {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|en}}[[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]][[ja:日吉大社]] and {{ill|Enryaku-ji|en}}[[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]][[ja:延暦寺]]. At {{ill|Guoqing Temple|en}}[[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]][[ja:国清寺]] on {{ill|Mount Tiantai|en}}[[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]][[ja:天台山]], where {{ill|Saichō|en}}[[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]][[ja:最澄]] (Dengyō Daishi), the founder of Japanese Tendai Buddhism, studied, the guardian deity was '''Jizhu Sannō Yuanbi Zhenjun''' (地主山王元弼真君), a deified prince of {{ill|King Ling of Zhou|en}}[[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]][[ja:霊王_(周)]] in {{ill|Daoism|en}}[[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]][[ja:道教]]. When Saichō returned to Japan, he enshrined Sannō Gongen as the guardian deity of {{ill|Enryaku-ji|en}}[[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]][[ja:延暦寺]], modeling it after Guoqing Temple. The peak of {{ill|Otowa Mountain|en}}[[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]][[ja:音羽山_(滋賀県・京都府)]], known as Ushioyama in ancient times, was revered as a sacred place where households offered their first harvest to the gods[http://kanko.city.kyoto.lg.jp/detail.php?InforKindCode=1&ManageCode=1000415 Kyoto Tourism Navi - Hōgon-ji (Ushio Kannon, Ushioyama)]. This belief laid the foundation for the mountain worship of Hieyama (Mount Hiei). Additionally, the *Kojiki* mentions that [[Oyamakui no Kami]], also called Yamasue-no-Ōnushi-no-Kami, dwells on Mount Hiei (日枝山) and at {{ill|Matsuo Taisha|en}}[[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]][[ja:松尾大社]] in {{ill|Kadono District|en}}[[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]][[ja:葛野郡]]. Furthermore, from {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|en}}[[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]][[ja:大神神社]], where {{ill|Ōmononushi|en}}[[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]][[ja:大物主]] is worshipped as the *nigi-mitama* (gentle spirit) of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en}}[[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]][[ja:大己貴神]], the deity was enshrined at Mount Hiei. This process established {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|en}}[[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]][[ja:日吉大社]], making it one of the most syncretic shrines, serving as the guardian deity of the Tendai school and its temple complex[https://www.asahi.com/travel/hikyou/TKY201103270195.html "Japan's Tradition: Shinbutsu-shūgō (2)" - Asahi Shimbun Digital, March 28, 2011]. {{ill|Enryaku-ji|en}}[[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]][[ja:延暦寺]] and {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|en}}[[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]][[ja:日吉大社]] maintained a close relationship, with Enryaku-ji holding authority over appointments within Hiyoshi Taisha from the {{ill|Heian period|en}}[[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]][[ja:平安時代]] onward. As Tendai Buddhism spread throughout Japan, temples dedicated to Sannō Gongen, called '''Sannō-sha''', were established across the country. In addition to Sannō Gongen, Tendai Buddhism also enshrined {{ill|Hachioji Gongen|en}}[[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]][[ja:八王子権現]] at Mount Hiei and assigned {{ill|honji suijaku|en}}[[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]][[ja:本地垂迹]] Buddhas to the 21 Sannō Shrines. At the beginning of the {{ill|Edo period|en}}[[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]][[ja:江戸時代]], the shrine priests of Hiyoshi Taisha attempted to abolish Shinbutsu-shūgō and establish a form of exclusive Shinto known as {{ill|Yoshida Shintō|en}}[[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]][[ja:唯一神道|Yoshida Shintō=Yoshida Shintō|lt=Ikkō Shintō]]. This led them to burn Buddhist images and engage in an early attempt at {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|en}}[[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]][[ja:廃仏毀釈]], the movement to eliminate Buddhism. However, Enryaku-ji appealed to the shogunate, resulting in a court ruling against Hiyoshi Taisha. The shrine priests were exiled, and the shrine was placed under complete control of Enryaku-ji. During this period, Enryaku-ji established a rule that "any actions beyond festivals and cleaning must receive permission from Enryaku-ji". From that point on, Hiyoshi Taisha was also financially dependent on Enryaku-ji. == Separation of Kami and Buddhas / Haibutsu Kishaku == During the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en}}[[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]][[ja:明治維新]], the government implemented {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|en}}[[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]][[ja:神仏分離]], leading to the abolition of Sannō Gongen, the guardian deity of Tendai Buddhism. Hiyoshi Taisha was one of the first sites to undergo the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|en}}[[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]][[ja:神仏分離令]] in {{ill|Keiō|en}}[[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]][[ja:慶応]] 4 (1868). Hiyoshi Sannō Shrine was forcibly separated from Enryaku-jiBecause the Shinto faction engaged in aggressive destruction of Buddhist-related objects, the new government issued a directive on April 10, Keiō 4, to implement Shinbutsu Bunri more peacefully. and reorganized into {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|en}}[[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]][[ja:日吉大社]]. When the shrine attempted to take custody of its buildings' keys from Enryaku-ji, they were refused. In response, the shrine administrator, {{ill|Kiju Shigekuni|en}}[[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]][[ja:樹下茂国]], along with the Shinto guards and 100 hired farmers, stormed the shrine and destroyed 124 Buddhist statues, artifacts, and scriptures, while confiscating 48 bronze objects, including temple gongs. The seized metal was repurposed for cannon and currency production. This incident set a precedent for Haibutsu Kishaku across Japan. Today, many remaining {{ill|Sannō-sha|en}}[[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]][[ja:山王社]] shrines have become {{ill|Hie Shrines|en}}[[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]][[ja:日枝神社]] or {{ill|Hiyoshi Shrines|en}}[[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]][[ja:日吉神社]], enshrining {{ill|Ōyamakui-no-Kami|en}}[[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]][[ja:大山咋神]] as their principal deity. == Hie Sannō Mandala (日吉山王曼荼羅図) == '''Hie Sannō Mandala''' (日吉山王曼荼羅図) is a mandala depicting the Buddhas and deities enshrined at {{ill|Mount Hiei|en}}[[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]][[ja:比叡山]], composed according to the doctrines of {{ill|Tendai|en}}[[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]][[ja:天台宗]] Buddhism. Based on its format, it can be classified as a {{ill|Miya Mandala|en}}[[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]][[ja:宮曼荼羅]], {{ill|Honjibutsu Mandala|en}}[[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]][[ja:本地仏曼荼羅]], or {{ill|Suijaku Mandala|en}}[[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]][[ja:垂迹神曼荼羅]]. === Miya Mandala (宮曼荼羅図) === [[File:Sanno Miya Mandala.jpg|thumb|right|150px|Silk-colored Hie Sannō Miya Mandala (Muromachi period)
The upper section displays the twenty-one shrine deities, their honjibutsu (Buddhist counterparts), and their {{ill|seed syllables|en}}[[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]][[ja:種子 (密教)]]. The lower section provides a bird's-eye view of the twenty-one shrines, depicting two shrines at the summit of {{ill|Mount Hachioji|en}}[[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]][[ja:八王子山]] and the shrine complex at the foot of the mountain.]] The Miya Mandala is a depiction of shrine landscapes from an aerial perspective. These mandalas were created so that individuals unable to visit the shrine in person could worship from home as a substitute for pilgrimage. Examples designated as Important Cultural Properties include "Silk-colored Hie Sannō Miya Mandala" (held by the {{ill|Yamato Bunkakan|en}}[[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]][[ja:大和文華館]], Nara City) and "Silk-colored Hie Sannō Miya Mandala" (held by {{ill|Hyakusaiji Temple|en}}[[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]][[ja:百済寺]], Higashiomi City), as well as "Silk-colored Sannō Hōtō Mandala" (held by the {{ill|Otani University Museum|en}}[[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]][[ja:大谷大学博物館]], Kyoto City). === Honjibutsu Mandala (本地仏曼荼羅図) === The Honjibutsu Mandala illustrates the {{ill|Sannō Gongen|en}}[[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]][[ja:山王権現]] in their original Buddhist forms, depicting them seated as regular Buddhist statues within shrine buildings. === Suijaku Mandala (垂迹神曼荼羅図) === Contrasting with the Honjibutsu Mandala, the Suijaku Mandala depicts the {{ill|Sannō Gongen|en}}[[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]][[ja:山王権現]] in their Shinto god forms. It portrays a scene where the deities of Mount Hiei gather in a single shrine. Examples designated as Important Cultural Properties include the "Hie Sannō Suijaku Mandala" (held by {{ill|Saikyōji Temple|en}}[[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]][[ja:西教寺]], Otsu City), as well as "Silk-colored Hie Sannō Suijaku Mandala" (held by {{ill|Sannō Lecture|en}}[[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]][[ja:山王講]], Kurano-tsuji Tomokura) and "Silk-colored Hie Sannō Jūzenji Mandala" (held by {{ill|Shinnyo-en|en}}[[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]][[ja:真如苑]], Tokyo). == Honjibutsu of the 21 Sannō Shrines (山王21社の本地仏) == {| class="wikitable" ! !! [[sn:Kurikara Fudō]] [[sn:Kurikara sword]] [[sn:Kurikara sword]] [[zh:%E4%BF%B1%E5%88%A9%E4%BC%BD%E7%BE%85%E5%8A%8D]] [[it:Kurikara]]

{{redirect|Kurikara Fudō|Temples Known as "Kurikara Fudō"|Kurikara Fudō-ji}} {{記事名の制約|title=Kurikara Sword}} {{出典の明記|date=October 4, 2015 (Sun) 11:51 (UTC)}} [[File:Sword Dragon Kurikara (Nara National Museum).jpg|thumb|150px|Kurikara Dragon Sword with Two Child Deity Images, housed at the {{ill|Nara National Museum|ja|奈良国立博物館}}, Important Cultural Property]] [[ファイル:Kurikara Dragon Sutra Box.JPG|thumb|150px|Kurikara Dragon Maki-e Sutra Box, housed in the Okuninushi-in of [[Taimadera]] in Nara, National Treasure]] [[ファイル: Kurikara Doragon.jpg|150px|thumb|A framed depiction of the {{ill|Kurikara Dragon King|ja|倶利伽羅竜王}}]] '''Kurikara Sword''', also rendered '''Kurikara Sword''' (くりからけん) is the sword held in the right hand by the standing image of {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}}.<ref name="bank">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%80%B6%E5%88%A9%E4%BC%BD%E7%BE%85%E5%89%A3-1716677 |title=倶利伽羅剣(くりからけん) |publisher=Kotobank |accessdate=2017-01-17}}</ref> In the {{ill|Samaya form|ja|三昧耶形}}, it serves as the very symbol of {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}} and is regarded as the "Sword of Wisdom" that shatters the three poisons (greed, anger, and ignorance). The sword derives its name from the depiction of the {{ill|Kurikara Dragon King|ja|倶利伽羅竜王}} entwined by raging {{ill|flames|ja|炎}} around it.<ref name="bank" />
The {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}} in [[Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture]] houses a sword of the same name, which is recorded as a sacred treasure of the subsidiary shrine {{ill|Hakkengū|ja|別宮八剣宮}} in the "Chōshū Zasshi".<ref name="愛知">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/yukei/kougei/kensitei/0572.html |title=倶利迦羅剣（くりからけん） |publisher=Aichi Prefecture |accessdate=2017-01-17}}</ref> In [[1971]] (Showa 46), it was designated as a Cultural Property by Aichi Prefecture.<ref name="愛知" />
Additionally, a dish consisting of grilled {{ill|Unagi|ja|鰻}} wrapped around a bamboo skewer is sometimes named "Kurikara-yaki" (くりから焼き) after the sword, due to its resemblance in shape.<ref>{{Google books|yqrUDgAAQBAJ|美味い話にゃ肴あり 8巻|page=39}}, accessed November 13, 2021.</ref><ref>{{Google books|OaNBDwAAQBAJ|古地図片手に記者が行く 「鬼平犯科帳」から見える東京21世紀|page=244}}, accessed November 13, 2021.</ref>
== In Fiction == * In the Japanese manga {{ill|Blue Exorcist|ja|青の祓魔師}} (section: {{ill|道具|ja|道具}}), a weapon called the {{ill|Gōma Sword|ja|降魔剣}} appears. It is the principal image ({{ill|honzon|ja|本尊}}) of a fictional religion known as {{ill|Myōō Darani-shū|ja|明王陀羅尼宗}}, which venerates {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}}. According to the narrative, it once housed the spirit of {{ill|Garuda|ja|迦楼羅天|lt=Garuda|lt_ja=迦褸羅}} and vanquished the unclean king.

{{出典の明記|date=October 4, 2015 (Sun) 11:51 (UTC)}} [[File:Sword Dragon Kurikara (Nara National Museum).jpg|thumb|150px|Kurikara Dragon Sword with Two Child Deity Images, housed at the {{ill|Nara National Museum|ja|奈良国立博物館}}, Important Cultural Property]] [[File:Kurikara Dragon Sutra Box.JPG|thumb|150px|Kurikara Dragon Maki-e Sutra Box, housed in the Okuninushi-in of [[Taimadera]] in Nara, National Treasure]] [[File: Kurikara Doragon.jpg|150px|thumb|A framed depiction of the {{ill|Kurikara Dragon King|ja|倶利伽羅竜王}}]] '''Kurikara Sword''', (倶利伽羅剣) is the sword held in the right hand by the standing image of {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}}.<ref name="bank">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%80%B6%E5%88%A9%E4%BC%BD%E7%BE%85%E5%89%A3-1716677 |title=倶利伽羅剣(くりからけん) |publisher=Kotobank |accessdate=2017-01-17}}</ref> In the {{ill|Samaya form|ja|三昧耶形}}, it serves as the very symbol of {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}} and is regarded as the "Sword of Wisdom" that shatters the three poisons (greed, anger, and ignorance). The sword derives its name from the depiction of the {{ill|Kurikara Dragon King|ja|倶利伽羅竜王}} entwined by raging {{ill|flames|ja|炎}} around it.<ref name="bank" />
The {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}} in [[Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture]] houses a sword of the same name, which is recorded as a sacred treasure of the subsidiary shrine {{ill|Hakkengū|ja|別宮八剣宮}} in the "Chōshū Zasshi".<ref name="愛知">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/yukei/kougei/kensitei/0572.html |title=倶利迦羅剣（くりからけん） |publisher=Aichi Prefecture |accessdate=2017-01-17}}</ref> In [[1971]] (Showa 46), it was designated as a Cultural Property by Aichi Prefecture.<ref name="愛知" />
Additionally, a dish consisting of grilled {{ill|Unagi|ja|鰻}} wrapped around a bamboo skewer is sometimes named "Kurikara-yaki" (くりから焼き) after the sword, due to its resemblance in shape.<ref>{{Google books|yqrUDgAAQBAJ|美味い話にゃ肴あり 8巻|page=39}}, accessed November 13, 2021.</ref><ref>{{Google books|OaNBDwAAQBAJ|古地図片手に記者が行く 「鬼平犯科帳」から見える東京21世紀|page=244}}, accessed November 13, 2021.</ref>
== In Fiction == * In the Japanese manga {{ill|Blue Exorcist|ja|青の祓魔師}} (section: {{ill|道具|ja|道具}}), a weapon called the {{ill|Gōma Sword|ja|降魔剣}} appears. It is the principal image ({{ill|honzon|ja|本尊}}) of a fictional religion known as {{ill|Myōō Darani-shū|ja|明王陀羅尼宗}}, which venerates {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}}. According to the narrative, it once housed the spirit of {{ill|Garuda|ja|迦楼羅天|lt=Garuda|lt_ja=迦褸羅}} and vanquished the unclean king.

{{出典の明記|date=October 4, 2015 (Sun) 11:51 (UTC)}} [[File:Sword Dragon Kurikara (Nara National Museum).jpg|thumb|150px|Kurikara Dragon Sword with Two Child Deity Images, housed at the {{ill|Nara National Museum|ja|奈良国立博物館}}, Important Cultural Property]] [[File:Kurikara Dragon Sutra Box.JPG|thumb|150px|Kurikara Dragon Maki-e Sutra Box, housed in the Okuninushi-in of [[Taimadera]] in Nara, National Treasure]] [[File: Kurikara Doragon.jpg|150px|thumb|A framed depiction of the {{ill|Kurikara Dragon King|ja|倶利伽羅竜王}}]] '''Kurikara Sword''', (倶利伽羅剣) is the sword held in the right hand by the standing image of {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}}.<ref name="bank">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%80%B6%E5%88%A9%E4%BC%BD%E7%BE%85%E5%89%A3-1716677 |title=倶利伽羅剣(くりからけん) |publisher=Kotobank |accessdate=2017-01-17}}</ref> In the {{ill|Samaya form|ja|三昧耶形}}, it serves as the very symbol of {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}} and is regarded as the "Sword of Wisdom" that shatters the three poisons (greed, anger, and ignorance). The sword derives its name from the depiction of the {{ill|Kurikara Dragon King|ja|倶利伽羅竜王}} entwined by raging {{ill|flames|ja|炎}} around it.<ref name="bank" />
The {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}} in [[Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture]] houses a sword of the same name, which is recorded as a sacred treasure of the subsidiary shrine {{ill|Hakkengū|ja|別宮八剣宮}} in the "Chōshū Zasshi".<ref name="愛知">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/yukei/kougei/kensitei/0572.html |title=倶利迦羅剣（くりからけん） |publisher=Aichi Prefecture |accessdate=2017-01-17}}</ref> In [[1971]] (Showa 46), it was designated as a Cultural Property by Aichi Prefecture.<ref name="愛知" />
Additionally, a dish consisting of grilled {{ill|Unagi|ja|鰻}} wrapped around a bamboo skewer is sometimes named "Kurikara-yaki" (くりから焼き) after the sword, due to its resemblance in shape.<ref>{{Google books|yqrUDgAAQBAJ|美味い話にゃ肴あり 8巻|page=39}}, accessed November 13, 2021.</ref><ref>{{Google books|OaNBDwAAQBAJ|古地図片手に記者が行く 「鬼平犯科帳」から見える東京21世紀|page=244}}, accessed November 13, 2021.</ref>
== In Fiction == * In the Japanese manga {{ill|Blue Exorcist|ja|青の祓魔師}}, a weapon called the Kurikara<ref>[https://aonoexorcist.fandom.com/wiki/Kurikara Kurikara | Ao no Exorcist Wiki | Fandom]</ref> appears. It is the principal image ({{ill|honzon|ja|本尊}}) of a fictional religion known as {{ill|Myōō Dharani|ja|明王陀羅尼宗}}, which venerates {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}}. According to the narrative, it once housed the spirit of {{ill|Karura|ja|迦楼羅天|lt=Karura|lt_ja=迦褸羅}} and vanquished the unclean king.

revision.text:
{{redirect|Kurikara Fudō|Temples Known as "Kurikara Fudō"|{{ill|Kurikara Fudō-ji|ja|倶利迦羅不動寺}}}}
{{出典の明記|date=October 4, 2015 (Sun) 11:51 (UTC)}} [[File:Sword Dragon Kurikara (Nara National Museum).jpg|thumb|150px|Kurikara Dragon Sword with Two Child Deity Images, housed at the {{ill|Nara National Museum|ja|奈良国立博物館}}, Important Cultural Property]] [[File:Kurikara Dragon Sutra Box.JPG|thumb|150px|Kurikara Dragon Maki-e Sutra Box, housed in the Okuninushi-in of [[Taimadera]] in Nara, National Treasure]] [[File: Kurikara Doragon.jpg|150px|thumb|A framed depiction of the {{ill|Kurikara Dragon King|ja|倶利伽羅竜王}}]] '''Kurikara Sword''', (倶利伽羅剣) is the sword held in the right hand by the standing image of {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}}.<ref name="bank">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%80%B6%E5%88%A9%E4%BC%BD%E7%BE%85%E5%89%A3-1716677 |title=倶利伽羅剣(くりからけん) |publisher=Kotobank |accessdate=2017-01-17}}</ref> In the {{ill|Samaya form|ja|三昧耶形|1=Samaya Symbols|lt=Samaya form}}, it serves as the very symbol of {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}} and is regarded as the "Sword of Wisdom" that shatters the three poisons (greed, anger, and ignorance). The sword derives its name from the depiction of the {{ill|Kurikara Dragon King|ja|倶利伽羅竜王}} entwined by raging {{ill|flames|ja|炎}} around it.<ref name="bank" />
The {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}} in [[Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture]] houses a sword of the same name, which is recorded as a sacred treasure of the subsidiary shrine {{ill|Hakkengū|ja|別宮八剣宮}} in the "Chōshū Zasshi".<ref name="愛知">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/yukei/kougei/kensitei/0572.html |title=倶利迦羅剣（くりからけん） |publisher=Aichi Prefecture |accessdate=2017-01-17}}</ref> In [[1971]] (Showa 46), it was designated as a Cultural Property by Aichi Prefecture.<ref name="愛知" />
Additionally, a dish consisting of grilled {{ill|Unagi|ja|鰻}} wrapped around a bamboo skewer is sometimes named "Kurikara-yaki" (くりから焼き) after the sword, due to its resemblance in shape.<ref>{{Google books|yqrUDgAAQBAJ|美味い話にゃ肴あり 8巻|page=39}}, accessed November 13, 2021.</ref><ref>{{Google books|OaNBDwAAQBAJ|古地図片手に記者が行く 「鬼平犯科帳」から見える東京21世紀|page=244}}, accessed November 13, 2021.</ref>
== In Fiction == * In the Japanese manga {{ill|Blue Exorcist|ja|青の祓魔師}}, a weapon called the Kurikara<ref>[https://aonoexorcist.fandom.com/wiki/Kurikara Kurikara | Ao no Exorcist Wiki | Fandom]</ref> appears. It is the principal image ({{ill|honzon|ja|本尊}}) of a fictional religion known as {{ill|Myōō Dharani|ja|明王陀羅尼宗}}, which venerates {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}}. According to the narrative, it once housed the spirit of {{ill|Karura|ja|迦楼羅天|lt=Karura|lt_ja=迦褸羅}} and vanquished the unclean king.
== Footnotes == {{節stub|1=Footnote citations should be clearly specified|date=October 4, 2015 (Sun) 11:51 (UTC)}} {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== References == {{節stub|section=1|date=October 4, 2015 (Sun) 11:51 (UTC)}} <!-- In this section, please list the books and other materials that were actually consulted during the writing of the article -->

[[ja:日吉山王曼荼羅図]]
[[ja:山王権現]]
[[ja:山王権現]][[en:Sanno Gongen]]
[[sn:Kurikara Fudō]] [[sn:Kurikara sword]] [[sn:Kurikara sword]] [[zh:%E4%BF%B1%E5%88%A9%E4%BC%BD%E7%BE%85%E5%8A%8D]] [[it:Kurikara]]<noinclude>{{DEFAULTSORT:Kurikara sword}}</noinclude>

{{redirect|Kurikara Fudō|Temples Known as "Kurikara Fudō"|{{ill|Kurikara Fudō-ji|ja|倶利迦羅不動寺}}}}
{{出典の明記|date=October 4, 2015 (Sun) 11:51 (UTC)}} [[File:Sword Dragon Kurikara (Nara National Museum).jpg|thumb|150px|Kurikara Dragon Sword with Two Child Deity Images, housed at the {{ill|Nara National Museum|ja|奈良国立博物館}}, Important Cultural Property]] [[File:Kurikara Dragon Sutra Box.JPG|thumb|150px|Kurikara Dragon Maki-e Sutra Box, housed in the Okuninushi-in of [[Taimadera]] in Nara, National Treasure]] [[File: Kurikara Doragon.jpg|150px|thumb|A framed depiction of the {{ill|Kurikara Dragon King|ja|倶利伽羅竜王}}]] '''Kurikara Sword''', (倶利伽羅剣) is the sword held in the right hand by the standing image of {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}}.<ref name="bank">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%80%B6%E5%88%A9%E4%BC%BD%E7%BE%85%E5%89%A3-1716677 |title=倶利伽羅剣(くりからけん) |publisher=Kotobank |accessdate=2017-01-17}}</ref> In the {{ill|Samaya form|ja|三昧耶形|1=Samaya Symbols|lt=Samaya form}}, it serves as the very symbol of {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}} and is regarded as the "Sword of Wisdom" that shatters the three poisons (greed, anger, and ignorance). The sword derives its name from the depiction of the {{ill|Kurikara Dragon King|ja|倶利伽羅竜王}} entwined by raging {{ill|flames|ja|炎}} around it.<ref name="bank" />
The {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}} in [[Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture]] houses a sword of the same name, which is recorded as a sacred treasure of the subsidiary shrine {{ill|Hakkengū|ja|別宮八剣宮}} in the "Chōshū Zasshi".<ref name="愛知">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/yukei/kougei/kensitei/0572.html |title=倶利迦羅剣（くりからけん） |publisher=Aichi Prefecture |accessdate=2017-01-17}}</ref> In [[1971]] (Showa 46), it was designated as a Cultural Property by Aichi Prefecture.<ref name="愛知" />
Additionally, a dish consisting of grilled {{ill|Unagi|ja|鰻}} wrapped around a bamboo skewer is sometimes named "Kurikara-yaki" (くりから焼き) after the sword, due to its resemblance in shape.<ref>{{Google books|yqrUDgAAQBAJ|美味い話にゃ肴あり 8巻|page=39}}, accessed November 13, 2021.</ref><ref>{{Google books|OaNBDwAAQBAJ|古地図片手に記者が行く 「鬼平犯科帳」から見える東京21世紀|page=244}}, accessed November 13, 2021.</ref>
== In Fiction == * In the Japanese manga {{ill|Blue Exorcist|ja|青の祓魔師}}, a weapon called the Kurikara<ref>[https://aonoexorcist.fandom.com/wiki/Kurikara Kurikara | Ao no Exorcist Wiki | Fandom]</ref> appears. It is the principal image ({{ill|honzon|ja|本尊}}) of a fictional religion known as {{ill|Myōō Dharani|ja|明王陀羅尼宗}}, which venerates {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}}. According to the narrative, it once housed the spirit of {{ill|Karura|ja|迦楼羅天|lt=Karura|lt_ja=迦褸羅}} and vanquished the unclean king.

{{redirect|Kurikara Fudō|Temples Known as "Kurikara Fudō"|{{ill|Kurikara Fudō-ji|ja|倶利迦羅不動寺}}}}
{{出典の明記|date=October 4, 2015 (Sun) 11:51 (UTC)}} [[File:Sword Dragon Kurikara (Nara National Museum).jpg|thumb|150px|Kurikara Dragon Sword with Two Child Deity Images, housed at the {{ill|Nara National Museum|ja|奈良国立博物館}}, Important Cultural Property]] [[File:Kurikara Dragon Sutra Box.JPG|thumb|150px|Kurikara Dragon Maki-e Sutra Box, housed in the Okuninushi-in of [[Taimadera]] in Nara, National Treasure]] [[File: Kurikara Doragon.jpg|150px|thumb|A framed depiction of the {{ill|Kurikara Dragon King|ja|倶利伽羅竜王}}]] '''Kurikara Sword''', (倶利伽羅剣) is the sword held in the right hand by the standing image of {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}}.<ref name="bank">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%80%B6%E5%88%A9%E4%BC%BD%E7%BE%85%E5%89%A3-1716677 |title=倶利伽羅剣(くりからけん) |publisher=Kotobank |accessdate=2017-01-17}}</ref> In the {{ill|Samaya form|ja|三昧耶形|1=Samaya Symbols|lt=Samaya form}}, it serves as the very symbol of {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}} and is regarded as the "Sword of Wisdom" that shatters the three poisons (greed, anger, and ignorance). The sword derives its name from the depiction of the {{ill|Kurikara Dragon King|ja|倶利伽羅竜王}} entwined by raging {{ill|flames|ja|炎}} around it.<ref name="bank" />
The {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}} in [[Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture]] houses a sword of the same name, which is recorded as a sacred treasure of the subsidiary shrine {{ill|Hakkengū|ja|別宮八剣宮}} in the "Chōshū Zasshi".<ref name="愛知">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/yukei/kougei/kensitei/0572.html |title=倶利迦羅剣（くりからけん） |publisher=Aichi Prefecture |accessdate=2017-01-17}}</ref> In [[1971]] (Showa 46), it was designated as a Cultural Property by Aichi Prefecture.<ref name="愛知" />
Additionally, a dish consisting of grilled {{ill|Unagi|ja|鰻}} wrapped around a bamboo skewer is sometimes named "Kurikara-yaki" (くりから焼き) after the sword, due to its resemblance in shape.<ref>{{Google books|yqrUDgAAQBAJ|美味い話にゃ肴あり 8巻|page=39}}, accessed November 13, 2021.</ref><ref>{{Google books|OaNBDwAAQBAJ|古地図片手に記者が行く 「鬼平犯科帳」から見える東京21世紀|page=244}}, accessed November 13, 2021.</ref>
== In Fiction == * In the Japanese manga {{ill|Blue Exorcist|ja|青の祓魔師}}, a weapon called the Kurikara<ref>[https://aonoexorcist.fandom.com/wiki/Kurikara Kurikara | Ao no Exorcist Wiki | Fandom]</ref> appears. It is the principal image ({{ill|honzon|ja|本尊}}) of a fictional religion known as {{ill|Myōō Dharani|ja|明王陀羅尼宗}}, which venerates {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}}. According to the narrative, it once housed the spirit of {{ill|Karura|ja|迦楼羅天|lt=Karura|lt_ja=迦褸羅}} and vanquished the unclean king.

{{redirect|Kurikara Fudō|Temples Known as "Kurikara Fudō"|{{ill|Kurikara Fudō-ji|ja|倶利迦羅不動寺}}}}
{{出典の明記|date=October 4, 2015 (Sun) 11:51 (UTC)}} [[File:Sword Dragon Kurikara (Nara National Museum).jpg|thumb|150px|Kurikara Dragon Sword with Two Child Deity Images, housed at the {{ill|Nara National Museum|ja|奈良国立博物館}}, Important Cultural Property]] [[File:Kurikara Dragon Sutra Box.JPG|thumb|150px|Kurikara Dragon Maki-e Sutra Box, housed in the Okuninushi-in of [[Taimadera]] in Nara, National Treasure]] [[File: Kurikara Doragon.jpg|150px|thumb|A framed depiction of the {{ill|Kurikara Dragon King|ja|倶利伽羅竜王}}]] '''Kurikara Sword''', (倶利伽羅剣) is the sword held in the right hand by the standing image of {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}}.<ref name="bank">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%80%B6%E5%88%A9%E4%BC%BD%E7%BE%85%E5%89%A3-1716677 |title=倶利伽羅剣(くりからけん) |publisher=Kotobank |accessdate=2017-01-17}}</ref> In the {{ill|Samaya form|ja|三昧耶形|1=Samaya Symbols|lt=Samaya form}}, it serves as the very symbol of {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}} and is regarded as the "Sword of Wisdom" that shatters the three poisons (greed, anger, and ignorance). The sword derives its name from the depiction of the {{ill|Kurikara Dragon King|ja|倶利伽羅竜王}} entwined by raging {{ill|flames|ja|炎}} around it.<ref name="bank" />
The {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}} in [[Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture]] houses a sword of the same name, which is recorded as a sacred treasure of the subsidiary shrine {{ill|Hakkengū|ja|別宮八剣宮}} in the "Chōshū Zasshi".<ref name="愛知">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/yukei/kougei/kensitei/0572.html |title=倶利迦羅剣（くりからけん） |publisher=Aichi Prefecture |accessdate=2017-01-17}}</ref> In [[1971]] (Showa 46), it was designated as a Cultural Property by Aichi Prefecture.<ref name="愛知" />
Additionally, a dish consisting of grilled {{ill|Unagi|ja|鰻}} wrapped around a bamboo skewer is sometimes named "Kurikara-yaki" (くりから焼き) after the sword, due to its resemblance in shape.<ref>{{Google books|yqrUDgAAQBAJ|美味い話にゃ肴あり 8巻|page=39}}, accessed November 13, 2021.</ref><ref>{{Google books|OaNBDwAAQBAJ|古地図片手に記者が行く 「鬼平犯科帳」から見える東京21世紀|page=244}}, accessed November 13, 2021.</ref>
== In Fiction == * In the Japanese manga {{ill|Blue Exorcist|ja|青の祓魔師}}, a weapon called the Kurikara<ref>[https://aonoexorcist.fandom.com/wiki/Kurikara Kurikara | Ao no Exorcist Wiki | Fandom]</ref> appears. It is the principal image ({{ill|honzon|ja|本尊}}) of a fictional religion known as {{ill|Myōō Dharani|ja|明王陀羅尼宗}}, which venerates {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}}. According to the narrative, it once housed the spirit of {{ill|Karura|ja|迦楼羅天|lt=Karura|lt_ja=迦褸羅}} and vanquished the unclean king.
== Footnotes == {{節stub|1=Footnote citations should be clearly specified|date=October 4, 2015 (Sun) 11:51 (UTC)}}
{{Reflist}}

{{redirect|Kurikara Fudō|Temples Known as "Kurikara Fudō"|{{ill|Kurikara Fudō-ji|ja|倶利迦羅不動寺}}}}
{{出典の明記|date=October 4, 2015 (Sun) 11:51 (UTC)}} [[File:Sword Dragon Kurikara (Nara National Museum).jpg|thumb|150px|Kurikara Dragon Sword with Two Child Deity Images, housed at the {{ill|Nara National Museum|ja|奈良国立博物館}}, Important Cultural Property]] [[File:Kurikara Dragon Sutra Box.JPG|thumb|150px|Kurikara Dragon Maki-e Sutra Box, housed in the Okuninushi-in of [[Taimadera]] in Nara, National Treasure]] [[File: Kurikara Doragon.jpg|150px|thumb|A framed depiction of the {{ill|Kurikara Dragon King|ja|倶利伽羅竜王}}]] '''Kurikara Sword''', (倶利伽羅剣) is the sword held in the right hand by the standing image of {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}}.<ref name="bank">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%80%B6%E5%88%A9%E4%BC%BD%E7%BE%85%E5%89%A3-1716677 |title=倶利伽羅剣(くりからけん) |publisher=Kotobank |accessdate=2017-01-17}}</ref> In the {{ill|Samaya form|ja|三昧耶形|1=Samaya Symbols|lt=Samaya form}}, it serves as the very symbol of {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}} and is regarded as the "Sword of Wisdom" that shatters the three poisons (greed, anger, and ignorance). The sword derives its name from the depiction of the {{ill|Kurikara Dragon King|ja|倶利伽羅竜王}} entwined by raging {{ill|flames|ja|炎}} around it.<ref name="bank" />
The {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}} in [[Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture]] houses a sword of the same name, which is recorded as a sacred treasure of the subsidiary shrine {{ill|Hakkengū|ja|別宮八剣宮}} in the "Chōshū Zasshi".<ref name="愛知">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/yukei/kougei/kensitei/0572.html |title=倶利迦羅剣（くりからけん） |publisher=Aichi Prefecture |accessdate=2017-01-17}}</ref> In [[1971]] (Showa 46), it was designated as a Cultural Property by Aichi Prefecture.<ref name="愛知" />
Additionally, a dish consisting of grilled {{ill|Unagi|ja|鰻}} wrapped around a bamboo skewer is sometimes named "Kurikara-yaki" (くりから焼き) after the sword, due to its resemblance in shape.<ref>{{Google books|yqrUDgAAQBAJ|美味い話にゃ肴あり 8巻|page=39}}, accessed November 13, 2021.</ref><ref>{{Google books|OaNBDwAAQBAJ|古地図片手に記者が行く 「鬼平犯科帳」から見える東京21世紀|page=244}}, accessed November 13, 2021.</ref>
== In Fiction == * In the Japanese manga {{ill|Blue Exorcist|ja|青の祓魔師}}, a weapon called the Kurikara<ref>[https://aonoexorcist.fandom.com/wiki/Kurikara Kurikara | Ao no Exorcist Wiki | Fandom]</ref> appears. It is the principal image ({{ill|honzon|ja|本尊}}) of a fictional religion known as {{ill|Myōō Dharani|ja|明王陀羅尼宗}}, which venerates {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}}. According to the narrative, it once housed the spirit of {{ill|Karura|ja|迦楼羅天|lt=Karura|lt_ja=迦褸羅}} and vanquished the unclean king.
== Footnotes == {{節stub|1=Footnote citations should be clearly specified|date=October 4, 2015 (Sun) 11:51 (UTC)}}

[[File:Sword Dragon Kurikara (Nara National Museum).jpg|thumb|150px|Kurikara Dragon Sword with Two Child Deity Images, housed at the [[:en:Nara National Museum|Nara National Museum]], Important Cultural Property]]
[[File:Kurikara Dragon Sutra Box.JPG|thumb|150px|Kurikara Dragon Maki-e Sutra Box, housed in the Okuninushi-in of [[:en:Taimadera|Taimadera]] in Nara, National Treasure]]
[[File: Kurikara Doragon.jpg|150px|thumb|A framed depiction of the {{ill|Kurikara Dragon King|ja|倶利伽羅竜王|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Dragon King|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 倶利伽羅}}]]
'''Kurikara Sword''', (倶利伽羅剣) is the sword held in the right hand by the standing image of [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]].<ref name="bank">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%80%B6%E5%88%A9%E4%BC%BD%E7%BE%85%E5%89%A3-1716677 |title=倶利伽羅剣(くりからけん) |publisher=Kotobank |accessdate=2017-01-17}}</ref> In the {{ill|Samaya form|ja|三昧耶形|1=Samaya Symbols|lt=Samaya form|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samaya form|13=User:Immanuelle/Samaya Symbols|qq=}}, it serves as the very symbol of [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]] and is regarded as the "Sword of Wisdom" that shatters the three poisons (greed, anger, and ignorance). The sword derives its name from the depiction of the {{ill|Kurikara Dragon King|ja|倶利伽羅竜王|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Dragon King|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 倶利伽羅}} entwined by raging [[:en:Flame|flames]] around it.<ref name="bank" />

The [[:en:Atsuta Shrine|Atsuta Shrine]] in [[:en:Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture|Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture]] houses a sword of the same name, which is recorded as a sacred treasure of the subsidiary shrine {{ill|Hakkengū|ja|別宮八剣宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakkengū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八剣宮}} in the "Chōshū Zasshi".<ref name="愛知">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/yukei/kougei/kensitei/0572.html |title=倶利迦羅剣（くりからけん） |publisher=Aichi Prefecture |accessdate=2017-01-17}}</ref> In [[:en:1971|1971]] (Showa 46), it was designated as a Cultural Property by Aichi Prefecture.<ref name="愛知" />

Additionally, a dish consisting of grilled {{ill|Unagi|ja|鰻|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウナギ|1=unagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1422437}} wrapped around a bamboo skewer is sometimes named "Kurikara-yaki" (くりから焼き) after the sword, due to its resemblance in shape.<ref>{{Google books|yqrUDgAAQBAJ|美味い話にゃ肴あり 8巻|page=39}}, accessed November 13, 2021.</ref><ref>{{Google books|OaNBDwAAQBAJ|古地図片手に記者が行く 「鬼平犯科帳」から見える東京21世紀|page=244}}, accessed November 13, 2021.</ref>

== In Fiction ==
* In the Japanese manga [[:en:Blue Exorcist|Blue Exorcist]], a weapon called the Kurikara<ref>[https://aonoexorcist.fandom.com/wiki/Kurikara Kurikara | Ao no Exorcist Wiki | Fandom]</ref> appears. It is the principal image ([[:en:Honzon|honzon]]) of a fictional religion known as {{ill|Myōō Dharani|ja|明王陀羅尼宗|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōō Dharani|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which venerates [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]]. According to the narrative, it once housed the spirit of {{ill|Karura|ja|迦楼羅天|lt=Karura|lt_ja=迦褸羅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karura|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 迦楼羅|1=Karura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q82198}} and vanquished the unclean king.

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none)
Wikidata name: (none)
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:19, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none)
Wikidata name: (none)
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:45, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{redirect|Kurikara Fudō|Temples Known as "Kurikara Fudō"|{{ill|Kurikara Fudō-ji|ja|倶利迦羅不動寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Fudō-ji|qq=}}}}

{{Referencesの明記|date=October 4, 2015 (Sun) 11:51 (UTC)}}
[[File:Sword Dragon Kurikara (Nara National Museum).jpg|thumb|150px|Kurikara Dragon Sword with Two Child Deity Images, housed at the [[:en:Nara National Museum|Nara National Museum]], Important Cultural Property]]
[[File:Kurikara Dragon Sutra Box.JPG|thumb|150px|Kurikara Dragon Maki-e Sutra Box, housed in the Okuninushi-in of [[:en:Taimadera|Taimadera]] in Nara, National Treasure]]
[[File: Kurikara Doragon.jpg|150px|thumb|A framed depiction of the {{ill|Kurikara Dragon King|ja|倶利伽羅竜王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Dragon King|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 倶利伽羅}}]]
'''Kurikara Sword''', (倶利伽羅剣) is the sword held in the right hand by the standing image of [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]].<ref name="bank">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%80%B6%E5%88%A9%E4%BC%BD%E7%BE%85%E5%89%A3-1716677 |title=倶利伽羅剣(くりからけん) |publisher=Kotobank |accessdate=2017-01-17}}</ref> In the {{ill|Samaya form|ja|三昧耶形|1=Samaya Symbols|lt=Samaya form|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samaya form|13=User:Immanuelle/Samaya Symbols|qq=}}, it serves as the very symbol of [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]] and is regarded as the "Sword of Wisdom" that shatters the three poisons (greed, anger, and ignorance). The sword derives its name from the depiction of the {{ill|Kurikara Dragon King|ja|倶利伽羅竜王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Dragon King|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 倶利伽羅}} entwined by raging [[:en:Flame|flames]] around it.<ref name="bank" />

The [[:en:Atsuta Shrine|Atsuta Shrine]] in [[:en:Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture|Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture]] houses a sword of the same name, which is recorded as a sacred treasure of the subsidiary shrine {{ill|Hakkengū|ja|別宮八剣宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakkengū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八剣宮}} in the "Chōshū Zasshi".<ref name="愛知">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/yukei/kougei/kensitei/0572.html |title=倶利迦羅剣（くりからけん） |publisher=Aichi Prefecture |accessdate=2017-01-17}}</ref> In [[:en:1971|1971]] (Showa 46), it was designated as a Cultural Property by Aichi Prefecture.<ref name="愛知" />

Additionally, a dish consisting of grilled {{ill|Unagi|ja|鰻|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウナギ|1=unagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1422437}} wrapped around a bamboo skewer is sometimes named "Kurikara-yaki" (くりから焼き) after the sword, due to its resemblance in shape.<ref>{{Google books|yqrUDgAAQBAJ|美味い話にゃ肴あり 8巻|page=39}}, accessed November 13, 2021.</ref><ref>{{Google books|OaNBDwAAQBAJ|古地図片手に記者が行く 「鬼平犯科帳」から見える東京21世紀|page=244}}, accessed November 13, 2021.</ref>

== In Fiction ==
* In the Japanese manga [[:en:Blue Exorcist|Blue Exorcist]], a weapon called the Kurikara<ref>[https://aonoexorcist.fandom.com/wiki/Kurikara Kurikara | Ao no Exorcist Wiki | Fandom]</ref> appears. It is the principal image ([[:en:Honzon|honzon]]) of a fictional religion known as {{ill|Myōō Dharani|ja|明王陀羅尼宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōō Dharani|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which venerates [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]]. According to the narrative, it once housed the spirit of {{ill|Karura|ja|迦楼羅天|lt=Karura|lt_ja=迦褸羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karura|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 迦楼羅|1=Karura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q82198}} and vanquished the unclean king.

== Footnotes ==

{{Reflist}}

{{redirect|Kurikara Fudō|Temples Known as "Kurikara Fudō"|{{ill|Kurikara Fudō-ji|ja|倶利迦羅不動寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Fudō-ji|qq=}}}}

{{Referencesの明記|date=October 4, 2015 (Sun) 11:51 (UTC)}}
[[File:Sword Dragon Kurikara (Nara National Museum).jpg|thumb|150px|Kurikara Dragon Sword with Two Child Deity Images, housed at the [[:en:Nara National Museum|Nara National Museum]], Important Cultural Property]]
[[File:Kurikara Dragon Sutra Box.JPG|thumb|150px|Kurikara Dragon Maki-e Sutra Box, housed in the Okuninushi-in of [[:en:Taimadera|Taimadera]] in Nara, National Treasure]]
[[File: Kurikara Doragon.jpg|150px|thumb|A framed depiction of the {{ill|Kurikara Dragon King|ja|倶利伽羅竜王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Dragon King|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 倶利伽羅}}]]
'''Kurikara Sword''', (倶利伽羅剣) is the sword held in the right hand by the standing image of [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]].<ref name="bank">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%80%B6%E5%88%A9%E4%BC%BD%E7%BE%85%E5%89%A3-1716677 |title=倶利伽羅剣(くりからけん) |publisher=Kotobank |accessdate=2017-01-17}}</ref> In the {{ill|Samaya form|ja|三昧耶形|1=Samaya Symbols|lt=Samaya form|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samaya form|13=User:Immanuelle/Samaya Symbols|qq=}}, it serves as the very symbol of [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]] and is regarded as the "Sword of Wisdom" that shatters the three poisons (greed, anger, and ignorance). The sword derives its name from the depiction of the {{ill|Kurikara Dragon King|ja|倶利伽羅竜王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Dragon King|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 倶利伽羅}} entwined by raging [[:en:Flame|flames]] around it.<ref name="bank" />

The [[:en:Atsuta Shrine|Atsuta Shrine]] in [[:en:Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture|Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture]] houses a sword of the same name, which is recorded as a sacred treasure of the subsidiary shrine {{ill|Hakkengū|ja|別宮八剣宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakkengū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八剣宮}} in the "Chōshū Zasshi".<ref name="愛知">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/yukei/kougei/kensitei/0572.html |title=倶利迦羅剣（くりからけん） |publisher=Aichi Prefecture |accessdate=2017-01-17}}</ref> In [[:en:1971|1971]] (Showa 46), it was designated as a Cultural Property by Aichi Prefecture.<ref name="愛知" />

Additionally, a dish consisting of grilled {{ill|Unagi|ja|鰻|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウナギ|1=unagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1422437}} wrapped around a bamboo skewer is sometimes named "Kurikara-yaki" (くりから焼き) after the sword, due to its resemblance in shape.<ref>{{Google books|yqrUDgAAQBAJ|美味い話にゃ肴あり 8巻|page=39}}, accessed November 13, 2021.</ref><ref>{{Google books|OaNBDwAAQBAJ|古地図片手に記者が行く 「鬼平犯科帳」から見える東京21世紀|page=244}}, accessed November 13, 2021.</ref>

== In Fiction ==
* In the Japanese manga [[:en:Blue Exorcist|Blue Exorcist]], a weapon called the Kurikara<ref>[https://aonoexorcist.fandom.com/wiki/Kurikara Kurikara | Ao no Exorcist Wiki | Fandom]</ref> appears. It is the principal image ([[:en:Honzon|honzon]]) of a fictional religion known as {{ill|Myōō Dharani|ja|明王陀羅尼宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōō Dharani|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which venerates [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]]. According to the narrative, it once housed the spirit of {{ill|Karura|ja|迦楼羅天|lt=Karura|lt_ja=迦褸羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karura|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 迦楼羅|1=Karura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q82198}} and vanquished the unclean king.

Additionally, a dish consisting of grilled {{ill|Unagi|ja|鰻|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウナギ|1=unagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1422437}} wrapped around a bamboo skewer is sometimes named "Kurikara-yaki" (くりから焼き) after the sword, due to its resemblance in shape.<ref>{{Google books|yqrUDgAAQBAJ|美味い話にゃ肴あり 8巻|page=39}}, accessed November 13, 2021.</ref><ref>{{Google books|OaNBDwAAQBAJ|古地図片手に記者が行く 「鬼平犯科帳」から見える東京21世紀|page=244}}, accessed November 13, 2021.</ref>
== In Fiction == * In the Japanese manga [[:en:Blue Exorcist|Blue Exorcist]], a weapon called the Kurikara<ref>[https://aonoexorcist.fandom.com/wiki/Kurikara Kurikara | Ao no Exorcist Wiki | Fandom]</ref> appears. It is the principal image ([[:en:Honzon|honzon]]) of a fictional religion known as {{ill|Myōō Dharani|ja|明王陀羅尼宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōō Dharani|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which venerates [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]]. According to the narrative, it once housed the spirit of {{ill|Karura|ja|迦楼羅天|lt=Karura|lt_ja=迦褸羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karura|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 迦楼羅|1=Karura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q82198}} and vanquished the unclean king.

{{Referencesの明記|date=October 4, 2015 (Sun) 11:51 (UTC)}}
[[File:Sword Dragon Kurikara (Nara National Museum).jpg|thumb|150px|Kurikara Dragon Sword with Two Child Deity Images, housed at the [[:en:Nara National Museum|Nara National Museum]], Important Cultural Property]]
[[File:Kurikara Dragon Sutra Box.JPG|thumb|150px|Kurikara Dragon Maki-e Sutra Box, housed in the Okuninushi-in of [[:en:Taimadera|Taimadera]] in Nara, National Treasure]]
[[File: Kurikara Doragon.jpg|150px|thumb|A framed depiction of the {{ill|Kurikara Dragon King|ja|倶利伽羅竜王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Dragon King|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 倶利伽羅}}]]
'''Kurikara Sword''', (倶利伽羅剣) is the sword held in the right hand by the standing image of [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]].<ref name="bank">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%80%B6%E5%88%A9%E4%BC%BD%E7%BE%85%E5%89%A3-1716677 |title=倶利伽羅剣(くりからけん) |publisher=Kotobank |accessdate=2017-01-17}}</ref> In the {{ill|Samaya form|ja|三昧耶形|1=Samaya Symbols|lt=Samaya form|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samaya form|13=User:Immanuelle/Samaya Symbols|qq=}}, it serves as the very symbol of [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]] and is regarded as the "Sword of Wisdom" that shatters the three poisons (greed, anger, and ignorance). The sword derives its name from the depiction of the {{ill|Kurikara Dragon King|ja|倶利伽羅竜王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Dragon King|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 倶利伽羅}} entwined by raging [[:en:Flame|flames]] around it.<ref name="bank" />

The [[:en:Atsuta Shrine|Atsuta Shrine]] in [[:en:Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture|Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture]] houses a sword of the same name, which is recorded as a sacred treasure of the subsidiary shrine {{ill|Hakkengū|ja|別宮八剣宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakkengū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八剣宮}} in the "Chōshū Zasshi".<ref name="愛知">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/yukei/kougei/kensitei/0572.html |title=倶利迦羅剣（くりからけん） |publisher=Aichi Prefecture |accessdate=2017-01-17}}</ref> In [[:en:1971|1971]] (Showa 46), it was designated as a Cultural Property by Aichi Prefecture.<ref name="愛知" />

Additionally, a dish consisting of grilled {{ill|Unagi|ja|鰻|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウナギ|1=unagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1422437}} wrapped around a bamboo skewer is sometimes named "Kurikara-yaki" (くりから焼き) after the sword, due to its resemblance in shape.<ref>{{Google books|yqrUDgAAQBAJ|美味い話にゃ肴あり 8巻|page=39}}, accessed November 13, 2021.</ref><ref>{{Google books|OaNBDwAAQBAJ|古地図片手に記者が行く 「鬼平犯科帳」から見える東京21世紀|page=244}}, accessed November 13, 2021.</ref>

== In Fiction ==
* In the Japanese manga [[:en:Blue Exorcist|Blue Exorcist]], a weapon called the Kurikara<ref>[https://aonoexorcist.fandom.com/wiki/Kurikara Kurikara | Ao no Exorcist Wiki | Fandom]</ref> appears. It is the principal image ([[:en:Honzon|honzon]]) of a fictional religion known as {{ill|Myōō Dharani|ja|明王陀羅尼宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōō Dharani|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which venerates [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]]. According to the narrative, it once housed the spirit of {{ill|Karura|ja|迦楼羅天|lt=Karura|lt_ja=迦褸羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karura|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 迦楼羅|1=Karura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q82198}} and vanquished the unclean king.

[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:45, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{redirect|Kurikara Fudō|Temples Known as "Kurikara Fudō"|{{ill|Kurikara Fudō-ji|ja|倶利迦羅不動寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Fudō-ji|qq=}}}}

{{Referencesの明記|date=October 4, 2015 (Sun) 11:51 (UTC)}}
[[File:Sword Dragon Kurikara (Nara National Museum).jpg|thumb|150px|Kurikara Dragon Sword with Two Child Deity Images, housed at the [[:en:Nara National Museum|Nara National Museum]], Important Cultural Property]]
[[File:Kurikara Dragon Sutra Box.JPG|thumb|150px|Kurikara Dragon Maki-e Sutra Box, housed in the Okuninushi-in of [[:en:Taimadera|Taimadera]] in Nara, National Treasure]]
[[File: Kurikara Doragon.jpg|150px|thumb|A framed depiction of the {{ill|Kurikara Dragon King|ja|倶利伽羅竜王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Dragon King|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 倶利伽羅}}]]
'''Kurikara Sword''', (倶利伽羅剣) is the sword held in the right hand by the standing image of [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]].<ref name="bank">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%80%B6%E5%88%A9%E4%BC%BD%E7%BE%85%E5%89%A3-1716677 |title=倶利伽羅剣(くりからけん) |publisher=Kotobank |accessdate=2017-01-17}}</ref> In the {{ill|Samaya form|ja|三昧耶形|1=Samaya Symbols|lt=Samaya form|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samaya form|13=User:Immanuelle/Samaya Symbols|qq=}}, it serves as the very symbol of [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]] and is regarded as the "Sword of Wisdom" that shatters the three poisons (greed, anger, and ignorance). The sword derives its name from the depiction of the {{ill|Kurikara Dragon King|ja|倶利伽羅竜王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Dragon King|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 倶利伽羅}} entwined by raging [[:en:Flame|flames]] around it.<ref name="bank" />

The [[:en:Atsuta Shrine|Atsuta Shrine]] in [[:en:Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture|Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture]] houses a sword of the same name, which is recorded as a sacred treasure of the subsidiary shrine {{ill|Hakkengū|ja|別宮八剣宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakkengū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八剣宮}} in the "Chōshū Zasshi".<ref name="愛知">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/yukei/kougei/kensitei/0572.html |title=倶利迦羅剣（くりからけん） |publisher=Aichi Prefecture |accessdate=2017-01-17}}</ref> In [[:en:1971|1971]] (Showa 46), it was designated as a Cultural Property by Aichi Prefecture.<ref name="愛知" />

Additionally, a dish consisting of grilled {{ill|Unagi|ja|鰻|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウナギ|1=unagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1422437}} wrapped around a bamboo skewer is sometimes named "Kurikara-yaki" (くりから焼き) after the sword, due to its resemblance in shape.<ref>{{Google books|yqrUDgAAQBAJ|美味い話にゃ肴あり 8巻|page=39}}, accessed November 13, 2021.</ref><ref>{{Google books|OaNBDwAAQBAJ|古地図片手に記者が行く 「鬼平犯科帳」から見える東京21世紀|page=244}}, accessed November 13, 2021.</ref>

== In Fiction ==
* In the Japanese manga [[:en:Blue Exorcist|Blue Exorcist]], a weapon called the Kurikara<ref>[https://aonoexorcist.fandom.com/wiki/Kurikara Kurikara | Ao no Exorcist Wiki | Fandom]</ref> appears. It is the principal image ([[:en:Honzon|honzon]]) of a fictional religion known as {{ill|Myōō Dharani|ja|明王陀羅尼宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōō Dharani|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which venerates [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]]. According to the narrative, it once housed the spirit of {{ill|Karura|ja|迦楼羅天|lt=Karura|lt_ja=迦褸羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karura|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 迦楼羅|1=Karura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q82198}} and vanquished the unclean king.

== In Fiction == * In the Japanese manga [[:en:Blue Exorcist|Blue Exorcist]], a weapon called the Kurikara<ref>[https://aonoexorcist.fandom.com/wiki/Kurikara Kurikara | Ao no Exorcist Wiki | Fandom]</ref> appears. According to the narrative, it once housed the spirit of {{ill|Karura|ja|迦楼羅天|lt=Karura|lt_ja=迦褸羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karura|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 迦楼羅|1=Karura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q82198}} and vanquished the unclean king.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kurikara Sword]][[it:Kurikara]][[ja:倶利伽羅剣]][[zh:俱利伽羅劍]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kurikara Sword]][[it:Kurikara]][[ja:倶利伽羅剣]][[zh:俱利伽羅劍]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kurikara Sword]][[it:Kurikara]][[ja:倶利伽羅剣]][[zh:俱利伽羅劍]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:19, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:45, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{redirect|Kurikara Fudō|Temples Known as "Kurikara Fudō"|{{ill|Kurikara Fudō-ji|ja|倶利迦羅不動寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Fudō-ji|qq=}}}}
{{Referencesの明記|date=October 4, 2015 (Sun) 11:51 (UTC)}} [[File:Sword Dragon Kurikara (Nara National Museum).jpg|thumb|150px|Kurikara Dragon Sword with Two Child Deity Images, housed at the [[:en:Nara National Museum|Nara National Museum]], Important Cultural Property]] [[File:Kurikara Dragon Sutra Box.JPG|thumb|150px|Kurikara Dragon Maki-e Sutra Box, housed in the Okuninushi-in of [[:en:Taimadera|Taimadera]] in Nara, National Treasure]] [[File: Kurikara Doragon.jpg|150px|thumb|A framed depiction of the {{ill|Kurikara Dragon King|ja|倶利伽羅竜王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Dragon King|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 倶利伽羅}}]] '''Kurikara Sword''', (倶利伽羅剣) is the sword held in the right hand by the standing image of [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]].<ref name="bank">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%80%B6%E5%88%A9%E4%BC%BD%E7%BE%85%E5%89%A3-1716677 |title=倶利伽羅剣(くりからけん) |publisher=Kotobank |accessdate=2017-01-17}}</ref> In the {{ill|Samaya form|ja|三昧耶形|1=Samaya Symbols|lt=Samaya form|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samaya form|13=User:Immanuelle/Samaya Symbols|qq=}}, it serves as the very symbol of [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]] and is regarded as the "Sword of Wisdom" that shatters the three poisons (greed, anger, and ignorance). The sword derives its name from the depiction of the {{ill|Kurikara Dragon King|ja|倶利伽羅竜王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Dragon King|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 倶利伽羅}} entwined by raging [[:en:Flame|flames]] around it.<ref name="bank" />
The [[:en:Atsuta Shrine|Atsuta Shrine]] in [[:en:Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture|Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture]] houses a sword of the same name, which is recorded as a sacred treasure of the subsidiary shrine {{ill|Hakkengū|ja|別宮八剣宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakkengū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八剣宮}} in the "Chōshū Zasshi".<ref name="愛知">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/yukei/kougei/kensitei/0572.html |title=倶利迦羅剣（くりからけん） |publisher=Aichi Prefecture |accessdate=2017-01-17}}</ref> In [[:en:1971|1971]] (Showa 46), it was designated as a Cultural Property by Aichi Prefecture.<ref name="愛知" />
Additionally, a dish consisting of grilled {{ill|Unagi|ja|鰻|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウナギ|1=unagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1422437}} wrapped around a bamboo skewer is sometimes named "Kurikara-yaki" (くりから焼き) after the sword, due to its resemblance in shape.<ref>{{Google books|yqrUDgAAQBAJ|美味い話にゃ肴あり 8巻|page=39}}, accessed November 13, 2021.</ref><ref>{{Google books|OaNBDwAAQBAJ|古地図片手に記者が行く 「鬼平犯科帳」から見える東京21世紀|page=244}}, accessed November 13, 2021.</ref>
== In Fiction == * In the Japanese manga [[:en:Blue Exorcist|Blue Exorcist]], a weapon called the Kurikara<ref>[https://aonoexorcist.fandom.com/wiki/Kurikara Kurikara | Ao no Exorcist Wiki | Fandom]</ref> appears. It is the principal image ([[:en:Honzon|honzon]]) of a fictional religion known as {{ill|Myōō Dharani|ja|明王陀羅尼宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōō Dharani|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which venerates [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]]. According to the narrative, it once housed the spirit of {{ill|Karura|ja|迦楼羅天|lt=Karura|lt_ja=迦褸羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karura|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 迦楼羅|1=Karura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q82198}} and vanquished the unclean king.

[[File:Sword Dragon Kurikara (Nara National Museum).jpg|thumb|150px|Kurikara Dragon Sword with Two Child Deity Images, housed at the {{ill|Nara National Museum|en|Nara National Museum|WD=Q147312|lt=Nara National Museum}}, Important Cultural Property]]
[[File:Kurikara Dragon Sutra Box.JPG|thumb|150px|Kurikara Dragon Maki-e Sutra Box, housed in the Okuninushi-in of [[:en:Taimadera|Taimadera]] in Nara, National Treasure]]
[[File: Kurikara Doragon.jpg|150px|thumb|A framed depiction of the {{ill|Kurikara Dragon King|ja|倶利伽羅竜王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Dragon King|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 倶利伽羅}}]]
'''Kurikara Sword''', (倶利伽羅剣) is the sword held in the right hand by the standing image of {{ill|Acala|en|Acala|WD=Q337624|lt=Fudō Myōō}}.<ref name="bank">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%80%B6%E5%88%A9%E4%BC%BD%E7%BE%85%E5%89%A3-1716677 |title=倶利伽羅剣(くりからけん) |publisher=Kotobank |accessdate=2017-01-17}}</ref> In the {{ill|Samaya form|ja|三昧耶形|1=Samaya Symbols|lt=Samaya form|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samaya form|13=User:Immanuelle/Samaya Symbols|qq=}}, it serves as the very symbol of {{ill|Acala|en|Acala|WD=Q337624|lt=Fudō Myōō}} and is regarded as the "Sword of Wisdom" that shatters the three poisons (greed, anger, and ignorance). The sword derives its name from the depiction of the {{ill|Kurikara Dragon King|ja|倶利伽羅竜王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Dragon King|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 倶利伽羅}} entwined by raging {{ill|Flame|en|Flame|WD=Q235544|lt=flames}} around it.<ref name="bank" />

The {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|Atsuta Shrine|WD=Q482065|lt=Atsuta Shrine}} in [[:en:Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture|Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture]] houses a sword of the same name, which is recorded as a sacred treasure of the subsidiary shrine {{ill|Hakkengū|ja|別宮八剣宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakkengū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八剣宮}} in the "Chōshū Zasshi".<ref name="愛知">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/yukei/kougei/kensitei/0572.html |title=倶利迦羅剣（くりからけん） |publisher=Aichi Prefecture |accessdate=2017-01-17}}</ref> In {{ill|1971|en|1971|WD=Q2475|lt=1971}} (Showa 46), it was designated as a Cultural Property by Aichi Prefecture.<ref name="愛知" />

Additionally, a dish consisting of grilled {{ill|Unagi|ja|鰻|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウナギ|1=unagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1422437}} wrapped around a bamboo skewer is sometimes named "Kurikara-yaki" (くりから焼き) after the sword, due to its resemblance in shape.<ref>{{Google books|yqrUDgAAQBAJ|美味い話にゃ肴あり 8巻|page=39}}, accessed November 13, 2021.</ref><ref>{{Google books|OaNBDwAAQBAJ|古地図片手に記者が行く 「鬼平犯科帳」から見える東京21世紀|page=244}}, accessed November 13, 2021.</ref>

== In Fiction ==
* In the Japanese manga {{ill|Blue Exorcist|en|Blue Exorcist|WD=Q495919|lt=Blue Exorcist}}, a weapon called the Kurikara<ref>[https://aonoexorcist.fandom.com/wiki/Kurikara Kurikara | Ao no Exorcist Wiki | Fandom]</ref> appears. It is the principal image ({{ill|Honzon|en|Honzon|WD=Q11520160|lt=honzon}}) of a fictional religion known as {{ill|Myōō Dharani|ja|明王陀羅尼宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōō Dharani|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which venerates {{ill|Acala|en|Acala|WD=Q337624|lt=Fudō Myōō}}. According to the narrative, it once housed the spirit of {{ill|Karura|ja|迦楼羅天|lt=Karura|lt_ja=迦褸羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karura|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 迦楼羅|1=Karura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q82198}} and vanquished the unclean king.

[[File: Kurikara Doragon.jpg|150px|thumb|A framed depiction of the {{ill|Kurikara Dragon King|ja|倶利伽羅竜王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Dragon King|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 倶利伽羅}}]]
'''Kurikara Sword''', (倶利伽羅剣) is the sword held in the right hand by the standing image of {{ill|Acala|en|Acala|WD=Q337624|lt=Fudō Myōō}}.<ref name="bank">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%80%B6%E5%88%A9%E4%BC%BD%E7%BE%85%E5%89%A3-1716677 |title=倶利伽羅剣(くりからけん) |publisher=Kotobank |accessdate=2017-01-17}}</ref> In the {{ill|Samaya form|ja|三昧耶形|1=Samaya Symbols|lt=Samaya form|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samaya form|13=User:Immanuelle/Samaya Symbols|qq=}}, it serves as the very symbol of {{ill|Acala|en|Acala|WD=Q337624|lt=Fudō Myōō}} and is regarded as the "Sword of Wisdom" that shatters the three poisons (greed, anger, and ignorance). The sword derives its name from the depiction of the {{ill|Kurikara Dragon King|ja|倶利伽羅竜王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Dragon King|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 倶利伽羅}} entwined by raging {{ill|Flame|en|Flame|WD=Q235544|lt=flames}} around it.<ref name="bank" />

The {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|Atsuta Shrine|WD=Q482065|lt=Atsuta Shrine}} in [[:en:Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture|Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture]] houses a sword of the same name, which is recorded as a sacred treasure of the subsidiary shrine {{ill|Hakkengū|ja|別宮八剣宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakkengū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八剣宮}} in the "Chōshū Zasshi".<ref name="愛知">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/yukei/kougei/kensitei/0572.html |title=倶利迦羅剣（くりからけん） |publisher=Aichi Prefecture |accessdate=2017-01-17}}</ref> In {{ill|1971|en|1971|WD=Q2475|lt=1971}} (Showa 46), it was designated as a Cultural Property by Aichi Prefecture.<ref name="愛知" />

Additionally, a dish consisting of grilled {{ill|Unagi|ja|鰻|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウナギ|1=unagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1422437}} wrapped around a bamboo skewer is sometimes named "Kurikara-yaki" (くりから焼き) after the sword, due to its resemblance in shape.<ref>{{Google books|yqrUDgAAQBAJ|美味い話にゃ肴あり 8巻|page=39}}, accessed November 13, 2021.</ref><ref>{{Google books|OaNBDwAAQBAJ|古地図片手に記者が行く 「鬼平犯科帳」から見える東京21世紀|page=244}}, accessed November 13, 2021.</ref>

== In Fiction ==
* In the Japanese manga {{ill|Blue Exorcist|en|Blue Exorcist|WD=Q495919|lt=Blue Exorcist}}, a weapon called the Kurikara<ref>[https://aonoexorcist.fandom.com/wiki/Kurikara Kurikara | Ao no Exorcist Wiki | Fandom]</ref> appears. It is the principal image ({{ill|Honzon|en|Honzon|WD=Q11520160|lt=honzon}}) of a fictional religion known as {{ill|Myōō Dharani|ja|明王陀羅尼宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōō Dharani|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which venerates {{ill|Acala|en|Acala|WD=Q337624|lt=Fudō Myōō}}. According to the narrative, it once housed the spirit of {{ill|Karura|ja|迦楼羅天|lt=Karura|lt_ja=迦褸羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karura|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 迦楼羅|1=Karura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q82198}} and vanquished the unclean king.

It is the principal image ({{ill|Honzon|en|Honzon|WD=Q11520160|lt=honzon|1=Honzon}}) of a fictional religion known as {{ill|Myōō Dharani|ja|明王陀羅尼宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōō Dharani|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which venerates {{ill|Acala|en|Acala|WD=Q337624|lt=Fudō Myōō|1=Acala}}. According to the narrative, it once housed the spirit of {{ill|Karura|ja|迦楼羅天|lt=Karura|lt_ja=迦褸羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karura|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 迦楼羅|1=Karura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q82198}} and vanquished the unclean king.

{{redirect|Kurikara Fudō|Temples Known as "Kurikara Fudō"|{{ill|Kurikara Fudō-ji|ja|倶利迦羅不動寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Fudō-ji|qq=}}}}

{{Referencesの明記|date=October 4, 2015 (Sun) 11:51 (UTC)}}
[[File:Sword Dragon Kurikara (Nara National Museum).jpg|thumb|150px|Kurikara Dragon Sword with Two Child Deity Images, housed at the {{ill|Nara National Museum|en|Nara National Museum|WD=Q147312|lt=Nara National Museum|1=Nara National Museum}}, Important Cultural Property]]
[[File:Kurikara Dragon Sutra Box.JPG|thumb|150px|Kurikara Dragon Maki-e Sutra Box, housed in the Okuninushi-in of [[:en:Taimadera|Taimadera]] in Nara, National Treasure]]
[[File: Kurikara Doragon.jpg|150px|thumb|A framed depiction of the {{ill|Kurikara Dragon King|ja|倶利伽羅竜王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Dragon King|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 倶利伽羅}}]]
'''Kurikara Sword''', (倶利伽羅剣) is the sword held in the right hand by the standing image of {{ill|Acala|en|Acala|WD=Q337624|lt=Fudō Myōō|1=Acala}}.<ref name="bank">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%80%B6%E5%88%A9%E4%BC%BD%E7%BE%85%E5%89%A3-1716677 |title=倶利伽羅剣(くりからけん) |publisher=Kotobank |accessdate=2017-01-17}}</ref> In the {{ill|Samaya form|ja|三昧耶形|1=Samaya Symbols|lt=Samaya form|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samaya form|13=User:Immanuelle/Samaya Symbols|qq=}}, it serves as the very symbol of {{ill|Acala|en|Acala|WD=Q337624|lt=Fudō Myōō|1=Acala}} and is regarded as the "Sword of Wisdom" that shatters the three poisons (greed, anger, and ignorance). The sword derives its name from the depiction of the {{ill|Kurikara Dragon King|ja|倶利伽羅竜王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Dragon King|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 倶利伽羅}} entwined by raging {{ill|Flame|en|Flame|WD=Q235544|lt=flames|1=Flame}} around it.<ref name="bank" />

The {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|Atsuta Shrine|WD=Q482065|lt=Atsuta Shrine|1=Atsuta Shrine}} in [[:en:Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture|Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture]] houses a sword of the same name, which is recorded as a sacred treasure of the subsidiary shrine {{ill|Hakkengū|ja|別宮八剣宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakkengū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八剣宮}} in the "Chōshū Zasshi".<ref name="愛知">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/yukei/kougei/kensitei/0572.html |title=倶利迦羅剣（くりからけん） |publisher=Aichi Prefecture |accessdate=2017-01-17}}</ref> In {{ill|1971|en|1971|WD=Q2475|lt=1971|1=1971}} (Showa 46), it was designated as a Cultural Property by Aichi Prefecture.<ref name="愛知" />

Additionally, a dish consisting of grilled {{ill|Unagi|ja|鰻|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウナギ|1=Unagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1422437}} wrapped around a bamboo skewer is sometimes named "Kurikara-yaki" (くりから焼き) after the sword, due to its resemblance in shape.<ref>{{Google books|yqrUDgAAQBAJ|美味い話にゃ肴あり 8巻|page=39}}, accessed November 13, 2021.</ref><ref>{{Google books|OaNBDwAAQBAJ|古地図片手に記者が行く 「鬼平犯科帳」から見える東京21世紀|page=244}}, accessed November 13, 2021.</ref>

== In Fiction ==
* In the Japanese manga {{ill|Blue Exorcist|en|Blue Exorcist|WD=Q495919|lt=Blue Exorcist|1=Blue Exorcist}}, a weapon called the Kurikara<ref>[https://aonoexorcist.fandom.com/wiki/Kurikara Kurikara | Ao no Exorcist Wiki | Fandom]</ref> appears. It is the principal image ({{ill|Honzon|en|Honzon|WD=Q11520160|lt=honzon|1=Honzon}}) of a fictional religion known as {{ill|Myōō Dharani|ja|明王陀羅尼宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōō Dharani|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which venerates {{ill|Acala|en|Acala|WD=Q337624|lt=Fudō Myōō|1=Acala}}. According to the narrative, it once housed the spirit of {{ill|Karura|ja|迦楼羅天|lt=Karura|lt_ja=迦褸羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karura|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 迦楼羅|1=Karura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q82198}} and vanquished the unclean king.

[[File: Kurikara Doragon.jpg|150px|thumb|A framed depiction of the {{ill|Kurikara Dragon King|ja|倶利伽羅竜王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Dragon King|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 倶利伽羅}}]]
'''Kurikara Sword''', (倶利伽羅剣) is the sword held in the right hand by the standing image of {{ill|Acala|en|Acala|WD=Q337624|lt=Fudō Myōō|1=Acala}}.<ref name="bank">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%80%B6%E5%88%A9%E4%BC%BD%E7%BE%85%E5%89%A3-1716677 |title=倶利伽羅剣(くりからけん) |publisher=Kotobank |accessdate=2017-01-17}}</ref> In the {{ill|Samaya form|ja|三昧耶形|1=Samaya Symbols|lt=Samaya form|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samaya form|13=User:Immanuelle/Samaya Symbols|qq=}}, it serves as the very symbol of {{ill|Acala|en|Acala|WD=Q337624|lt=Fudō Myōō|1=Acala}} and is regarded as the "Sword of Wisdom" that shatters the three poisons (greed, anger, and ignorance). The sword derives its name from the depiction of the {{ill|Kurikara Dragon King|ja|倶利伽羅竜王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Dragon King|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 倶利伽羅}} entwined by raging {{ill|Flame|en|Flame|WD=Q235544|lt=flames|1=Flame}} around it.<ref name="bank" />

The {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|Atsuta Shrine|WD=Q482065|lt=Atsuta Shrine|1=Atsuta Shrine}} in [[:en:Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture|Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture]] houses a sword of the same name, which is recorded as a sacred treasure of the subsidiary shrine {{ill|Hakkengū|ja|別宮八剣宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakkengū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八剣宮}} in the "Chōshū Zasshi".<ref name="愛知">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/yukei/kougei/kensitei/0572.html |title=倶利迦羅剣（くりからけん） |publisher=Aichi Prefecture |accessdate=2017-01-17}}</ref> In {{ill|1971|en|1971|WD=Q2475|lt=1971|1=1971}} (Showa 46), it was designated as a Cultural Property by Aichi Prefecture.<ref name="愛知" />

Additionally, a dish consisting of grilled {{ill|Unagi|ja|鰻|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウナギ|1=Unagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1422437}} wrapped around a bamboo skewer is sometimes named "Kurikara-yaki" (くりから焼き) after the sword, due to its resemblance in shape.<ref>{{Google books|yqrUDgAAQBAJ|美味い話にゃ肴あり 8巻|page=39}}, accessed November 13, 2021.</ref><ref>{{Google books|OaNBDwAAQBAJ|古地図片手に記者が行く 「鬼平犯科帳」から見える東京21世紀|page=244}}, accessed November 13, 2021.</ref>

== In Fiction ==
* In the Japanese manga {{ill|Blue Exorcist|en|Blue Exorcist|WD=Q495919|lt=Blue Exorcist|1=Blue Exorcist}}, a weapon called the Kurikara<ref>[https://aonoexorcist.fandom.com/wiki/Kurikara Kurikara | Ao no Exorcist Wiki | Fandom]</ref> appears. It is the principal image ({{ill|Honzon|en|Honzon|WD=Q11520160|lt=honzon|1=Honzon}}) of a fictional religion known as {{ill|Myōō Dharani|ja|明王陀羅尼宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōō Dharani|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which venerates {{ill|Acala|en|Acala|WD=Q337624|lt=Fudō Myōō|1=Acala}}. According to the narrative, it once housed the spirit of {{ill|Karura|ja|迦楼羅天|lt=Karura|lt_ja=迦褸羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karura|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 迦楼羅|1=Karura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q82198}} and vanquished the unclean king.
